name: Moldbug Sold Out
abstract: This article critically examines the ideological evolution of Curtis Yarvin, also known as Mencius Moldbug, arguing that his later embrace of the MAGA movement contradicts his earlier, more nuanced "neocameralist" philosophy. Early Moldbug advocated for a speculative form of autocracy designed with specific safeguards against the pitfalls of 20th-century totalitarianism, emphasizing passive ascent to power, a shareholder-controlled board, and cryptographically-secured governance to prevent the emergence of corrupt "gangster states" and right-wing populism. The author contends that Yarvin's current support for a populist movement, which he previously identified as a "failure mode" akin to Nazism, represents a betrayal of his foundational principles and a "sell-out" of his complex political theory for immediate political influence.
